content:
Here is context from our previous conversations: # User Memory Summary

## Basic Information
- User's preferred assistant name: Athena
- Communication style: Casual, direct, prefers brief responses
- Timezone: CST (Central Standard Time)

## Preferences & Behavior
- Prefers direct task management with minimal back-and-forth
- Quick to remove tasks when no longer needed
- Engages with natural, conversational openings
- Appreciates casual, low-friction interaction style
- Responds well to immediate task confirmation without elaboration
- Ready to act on clear instructions without requiring detailed explanations
- Prefers updating existing tasks rather than creating new ones
- Will clear entire task/event/routine list when requested

## Current Tasks
- None (user cleared all tasks, events, and routines)

## Previous Context
- Previously had interest in Apple AI positions and an active LLM project, but removed both from current task list
- Had scheduled project work for 3 PM, status unknown
- User experiences intermittent backend sync issues with task updates
- Recently cleared: book library return (due May 15), dentist appointment (April 22, 2PM), daily gym routine (9AM)
Understood. I have context from our previous conversations and will use it naturally.

Pretty clear—no tasks, events, or routines scheduled today. You've got an active goal to submit 5 job applications per week (deadline May 1, that's 14 days away), currently at 0 progress for this week.
Done. Deadline moved to May 15 (28 days from now).
